feed.title: Kubesimplify Blog
feed.subtitle: Deep dives on Kubernetes, AI infrastructure, GitOps, and the cloud-native stack, written by practitioners.

entry.title: Slicing GPUs in Kubernetes with NVIDIA Multi-Instance GPU (MIG)
entry.summary: Partition NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs into hardware-isolated MIG slices on Kubernetes: host-level slicing, GPU Operator automation, production pitfalls, and DCGM monitoring.

entry.title: Introducing kiac: Real Kubernetes Nodes on Your Mac, Each Its Own Lightweight VM
entry.summary: kiac runs local Kubernetes on macOS where every node is its own lightweight VM via apple/container: kubeadm or k3s flavors, Cilium on a custom kernel, built-in LoadBalancer, Grafana, Gateway API, and clusters that survive reboots.

entry.title: LLM Costs and Observability with agentgateway on Kubernetes (Part 2)
entry.summary: Part 2: scrape agentgateway with Prometheus, build a Grafana dashboard of token cost and per-tool usage, see blocked tool calls, and alert on spend.

entry.title: Controlling MCP Tools with agentgateway on Kubernetes (Part 1)
entry.summary: Run AI agents behind agentgateway on Kubernetes: route their LLM and MCP tool calls through one proxy, keep secrets out of the agent, and block tools by policy.

entry.title: Day 4: Quantization Demystified. BF16, FP8, NVFP4, MXFP4, INT4, GGUF, and Why It All Matters
entry.summary: A practical, beginner-friendly guide to BF16, FP8, NVFP4, MXFP4, INT4, and GGUF Q4_K_M on NVIDIA DGX Spark. Bytes per parameter, quality vs size, and which format to pick when.

entry.title: Day 3: The DGX Spark Unpacked. GB10, Unified Memory, sm_121, and the One Reason This Hardware Exists
entry.summary: A practical teardown of NVIDIA DGX Spark's GB10 Grace Blackwell Superchip, unified memory, sm_121, NVFP4 tensor cores, memory reporting, and decode limits.

entry.title: Wandler: Local OpenAI-Compatible Inference With Transformers.js and WebGPU
entry.summary: A practical Wandler deep dive with a local M1 Max WebGPU demo, real latency numbers, architecture diagrams, and getting-started commands.

entry.title: mlxcel: A Rust-Native Inference Engine for Apple Silicon, Tested on My M1 Max
entry.summary: Day-one deep dive into mlxcel v0.1.0, a Rust-native MLX inference engine. Real M1 Max benchmarks vs mlx-lm and Ollama on Llama 3.2 3B and Qwen 2.5 7B, with architecture diagrams and an honest take on TurboQuant.

entry.title: Day 2: Anatomy of an LLM Inference Request. From Prompt to Answer, Step by Step
entry.summary: A beginner-friendly walkthrough of tokenization, prefill, KV cache, decode, batching, TTFT, and why memory bandwidth shapes local LLM performance on NVIDIA DGX Spark.

entry.title: How kube-proxy Actually Works: iptables, IPVS, and nftables Inside Out
entry.summary: How kube-proxy turns Kubernetes Services into kernel rules. iptables, IPVS, nftables packet paths and which to pick in 2026. Verified against k/k 1.36 source.

entry.title: Day 1: The Local LLM Revolution. Why Your Desk Just Became the New Datacenter
entry.summary: Why local LLMs are becoming practical in 2026, what changed across open weights, hardware, and inference software, and why DGX Spark makes the desk feel like a small AI lab.

entry.title: What Actually Happens When You Run kubectl run nginx
entry.summary: Every step of what happens when you run kubectl run nginx on Kubernetes. From argv to etcd Raft, scheduler, CRI, CNI, runc, and PLEG.

entry.summary: Containers aren't VMs. They're Linux processes. A Docker Captain explains namespaces, cgroups, and how Docker really works on your Mac in 2026.

entry.summary: Learn how to set up Tailscale on your NVIDIA DGX Spark for secure SSH access from anywhere in the world.
